key_insight: Layered FeMN2 nitride thin films can be synthesized by reactive sputtering plus NH3 annealing, but FeWN2 and FeMoN2 accommodate composition very differently, leading to distinct texture, purity, and transport/magnetic behavior.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a composition-tuning route for designing layered nitride thin films with controllable microstructure and properties for electronic and magnetic applications.
method: Reactive sputtering of Fe–M precursors (M = W, Mo)
Post-deposition NH3 annealing to form layered FeMN2
Synchrotron GIWAXS and XAS to track phase, texture, and local coordination
evidence_signal: GIWAXS, XAS, resistivity, and magnetization measurements show broad-phase formation in both systems, but high-purity FeWN2 across compositions, a resistivity peak in FeMoN2 near stoichiometry, and weak ferromagnetic-like response only in Fe-poor FeWN2.
watch_item: Stoichiometry and cation substitution strongly affect phase purity and texture, so process windows may be narrow—especially for FeMoN2 near nominal composition.
paper_title: Composition-dependent Thin-film Synthesis of Layered Ternary Iron Nitrides FeMN2 (M = W, Mo)
